title: Refactorizar variantes: parametros RTL en QSF, eliminar hermeslite.v por variante
description:
Objetivo: Cada variante debe ser solo QSF + QPF + Makefile + README.md, sin hermeslite.v propio.

Patrón actual (Original): Cada variante tiene su propio hermeslite.v con parametros hardcoded (.NR(4), .NT(1), etc.) y un QSF que referencia ./hermeslite.v

Patrón deseado (referencia en ../../Hermes-Lite2):
1. Un unico hermeslite.v en rtl/ que usa ifdef/VERILOG_MACRO para parametrizar todo
2. Los parametros se definen en el QSF de cada variante como VERILOG_MACRO: HL2_NR, HL2_NT, HL2_UART, HL2_ATU, HL2_FAN, HL2_CW, HL2_HL2LINK, HL2_AK4951, HL2_EXTENDED_DEBUG_RESP, HL2_BYPASS_VERSA
3. El QSF referencia ../../rtl/hermeslite.v en lugar de ./hermeslite.v
4. Se eliminan los hermeslite.v individuales de cada variante
5. Se anade README.md a cada variante documentando los parametros

Subtareas:
a) Copiar el hermeslite.v unificado desde ../../Hermes-Lite2/gateware/rtl/hermeslite.v
b) Actualizar cada QSF: anadir VERILOG_MACRO con los parametros correctos (basandose en los valores del hermeslite.v actual de cada variante), cambiar referencia de ./hermeslite.v a ../../rtl/hermeslite.v
c) Eliminar los hermeslite.v individuales de cada variante
d) Crear README.md para cada variante
e) Verificar que los parametros coinciden con los valores originales de cada variante

Variantes a actualizar:
- hl2b2_main
- hl2b3to4_main
- hl2b3to4_cicrx
- hl2b5up_main
- hl2b5up_6rx
- hl2b5up_cicrx
- hl2b5up_15ce
- hl2b5up_4000
- hl2b5up_ak4951v3
- hl2b5up_ak4951v4
- hl2b5up_bypassversa

title: Copiar gateware HL2 desde Original a ea5iue
description: Copiar todo el arbol gateware/ desde el repo Original (softerhardware), excluyendo radioberry. Esto incluye: rtl/, boards/ (solo hl2*), variants/ (solo hl2*), bitfiles/, sim/. Los archivos de hardware y software no se copian ya que estan fuera del scope del gateware.

title: Document DB1 connector usage in every variant README
description:
Each variant README under gateware/variants/ must include a complete DB1 connector usage section. Currently only hl2b5up_main_iambic has partial DB1 documentation (sidetone on DB1-1). The other 13 variants lack DB1 info entirely.

**Scope**: Add a 'DB1 Connector Usage' section to the README.md of each of these 14 variants:
- hl2b2_main
- hl2b3to4_cicrx
- hl2b3to4_main
- hl2b5up_15ce
- hl2b5up_4000
- hl2b5up_6rx
- hl2b5up_ak4951v3
- hl2b5up_ak4951v3_yaesu
- hl2b5up_ak4951v4
- hl2b5up_ak4951v4_yaesu
- hl2b5up_bypassversa
- hl2b5up_cicrx
- hl2b5up_main
- hl2b5up_main_iambic (update existing section)

**Reference**: Full DB1 pinout is in docs/hl2_build9_connectors_signals_fpga_pins.md section 7. For each variant, document which DB1 pins are actively used by that variant's gateware, their function, and any special considerations (e.g. sidetone DAC on DB1-1 for iambic variant, I2C on pins 15-18 for companion board variants, LEDs on pins 9/11/13/14, etc.).

**Acceptance criteria**:
- Every variant README has a DB1 Connector Usage section
- Table lists only the pins actively used by that variant (not all 20 pins)
- Each pin entry shows: DB1 pin number, FPGA signal name, direction, function in this variant
- Reference link to the full DB1 pinout in docs/

title: Investigate GitHub Actions workflow for gateware release builds
description:
Research whether we can set up a GitHub Actions CI/CD workflow to compile all HL2 gateware variants and produce release artifacts (RBF/SVF/JAM files).

Consider:
1. Quartus availability in CI (Docker image from Intel, self-hosted runner on the Debian build machine)
2. Build matrix for all 14 HL2 variants
3. Artifact upload and release tagging
4. The build currently runs on a remote Debian machine via share
5. Evaluate if Docker+Quartus or self-hosted runner is the better approach
notes:
## Investigation Results

### Quartus Version
- We use **Quartus Prime 23.1std.1 Lite Edition** (Cyclone IV E support)

### Option A: Docker + GitHub Actions (hosted runners)

**Pros:**
- Reproducible, cloud-based, no infrastructure to maintain
- Docker images exist: `raetro/quartus` (tags 17.1-21.1.1, ~5-6GB), `intel/quartuspro-v24.2` (Pro only, no Lite)
- `halfmanhalftaco/fpga-docker` has Quartus 13.0-20.1.1 Dockerfiles
- Jamie Iles has a Dockerfile approach for Quartus Lite

**Cons:**
- **No pre-built Docker image for Quartus 23.1std.1 Lite** — would need to build our own (~10-20GB image)
- Intel only publishes **Pro** edition Docker images on hub.docker.com, not Lite
- Quartus Lite EULA may restrict redistribution of Docker images
- GitHub-hosted runners have 14GB temp storage — tight for Quartus + build artifacts
- Build time per variant: ~5-10 min. 14 variants = 70-140 min per build (billed time)
- Need to cache or pre-build the Docker image in GHCR to avoid 20GB downloads each run

**Feasibility: MEDIUM** — Possible but requires building and hosting a custom 23.1std.1 Lite Docker image. License gray area.

### Option B: Self-hosted runner on the Debian build machine

**Pros:**
- **Simplest approach** — the machine already has Quartus 23.1std.1 installed and working
- Zero licensing issues — Quartus runs on the machine as-is
- Full build power: can build all 14 variants in parallel (4 cores, or sequential)
- No Docker image to maintain
- Runner uses outbound-only HTTPS connection (no firewall changes needed)
- Setup: download, configure, register as systemd service — ~15 minutes
- Free (no GH Actions minutes consumed)

**Cons:**
- Machine must be online for builds to trigger
- Single point of failure (machine down = no CI)
- Security: runner executes arbitrary workflow code (mitigated by pinning workflow versions)
- No isolation — builds run in the host environment

**Feasibility: HIGH** — Ready to go with minimal setup.

### Option C: Hybrid — self-hosted runner + Docker

- Install runner on Debian machine but run Quartus inside Docker for isolation
- Best of both worlds but more complex to set up
- Overkill for this project

### Recommendation: **Option B (self-hosted runner)**

The Debian build machine already has Quartus 23.1std.1 installed. A self-hosted GitHub Actions runner can be set up in ~15 minutes and would immediately enable CI builds.

### Proposed Workflow Design

```yaml
name: Build Gateware
on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
    paths: ['gateware/**']
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      variant:
        description: 'Variant to build (or "all")'
        default: 'all'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: self-hosted
    strategy:
      matrix:
        variant:
          - hl2b2_main
          - hl2b3to4_main
          - hl2b3to4_cicrx
          - hl2b5up_main
          - hl2b5up_main_iambic
          - hl2b5up_6rx
          - hl2b5up_cicrx
          - hl2b5up_15ce
          - hl2b5up_ak4951v3
          - hl2b5up_ak4951v3_yaesu
          - hl2b5up_ak4951v4_yaesu
      fail-fast: false
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Build ${{ matrix.variant }}
        run: make -C gateware/variants/${{ matrix.variant }}
      - name: Upload artifacts
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: ${{ matrix.variant }}
          path: gateware/variants/${{ matrix.variant }}/build/

  release:
    needs: build
    runs-on: self-hosted
    if: startsWith(github.ref, 'refs/tags/')
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
      - name: Create release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
```

### Next Steps
1. Install self-hosted runner on Debian machine (`settings → Actions → Runners → New runner`)
2. Create `.github/workflows/build.yml` with the matrix workflow above
3. Test with a single variant first
4. Add release job triggered by tags

title: Commit inicial del gateware HL2 en repo ea5iue
description: Hacer commit de los archivos copiados con un mensaje descriptivo que indique la fuente (softerhardware/Hermes-Lite2) y la version (v74+).

title: Verificar que ji1udd commits ya estan en Original
description: Ya se ha verificado que el repo Original (softerhardware) ya contiene todos los commits de ji1udd (mismos parches, mismo autor, mismas fechas). Los commits cherry-picked incluyen: iambic Mode-B, CW duty fix, key-down delay, rx iq 8bit, AK4951 gain fix, sidetone module replacement, tx_gain init, build for 4K, y git ignores. No hace falta anadir nada de ji1udd.
